claims.verbatim_quote: the memoryless condition restricts its source distribution to be Dirac delta mu(x) := delta, precluding the use of common priors such as Gaussian or domain-specific priors such as the harmonic oscillators in molecular systems

claims.text: ASBS introduces a corrector-matching objective to correct nontrivial biases arising from non-memoryless conditions.
claims.verbatim_quote: It also introduces a corrector-matching objective to correct nontrivial biases arising from non-memoryless conditions.

claims.text: ASBS retains scalability advantages from AS by utilizing an adjoint-matching objective that removes the need for estimating target samples.
claims.verbatim_quote: ASBS retains all scalability advantages from AS by utilizing an adjoint-matching objective that removes the need for estimating target samples.

claims.text: On alanine dipeptide, ASBS achieves KL divergence of 0.02 on phi and 0.01 on psai torsions, significantly outperforming PIS, DDS, and AS.
claims.verbatim_quote: ASBS generates samples whose energy profiles closely match those of the ground-truth samples.

claims.text: ASBS implements alternating optimization between adjoint matching and corrector matching, equivalent to executing IPF and ensuring global convergence.
claims.verbatim_quote: alternating optimization between the two matching objectives is equivalent to executing the Iterative Proportional Fitting algorithm, ensuring global convergence of ASBS

claims.text: ASBS parametrizes models with Equivariant Graph Neural Networks and considers domain-specific harmonic prior for molecular systems.
claims.verbatim_quote: we parametrize the models with two Equivariant Graph Neural Networks and consider a domain-specific source distribution—the harmonic prior

claims.text: The CM objective solves a backward half bridge, forming the alternating IPF scheme with the forward AM objective.
claims.verbatim_quote: ASBS in Algorithm 1 implicitly employs an optimization scheme that alternates between solving forward and backward half bridges, thereby instantiating the celebrated Iterative Proportional Fitting algorithm

claims.text: The SOC problem in Adjoint Sampling is a specific case of the SB problem with (f_t, mu) := (0, delta), making ASBS a generalization.
claims.verbatim_quote: the SOC problem in AS corresponds to a specific case of the SB problem with (f_t, mu) := (0, delta), ASBS extends AS to handle non-memoryless conditions by solving more general SB problems

claims.text: ASBS uses replay buffers during training for improved stability, following standard practices from prior diffusion samplers.
claims.verbatim_quote: All models are trained with Adam and, following standard practices, utilize replay buffers

claims.text: The collapse deviation Delta measures the relative variation of normalized curves across model sizes
claims.verbatim_quote: We quantify the quality of collapse using the collapse deviation Delta, defined as: Delta(x) = V_{p,omega}[l(x,p,omega)]^{1/2} / E_{p,omega}[l(x,p,omega)]

claims.text: Prior diffusion-based methods often require importance-weighted estimation or complicated learning processes, trading off scalability with extensive evaluations.
claims.verbatim_quote: prior diffusion-based methods, known as diffusion samplers, often require importance-weighted estimation or complicated learning processes. Both trade off scalability with extensive evaluations of the energy and model, thereby limiting their practical usage.

claims.text: Pseudo-Huber loss used in improved CMs is a conditionally positive definite kernel matching all moments.
claims.verbatim_quote: Negative pseudo-huber loss is a conditionally positive definite kernel that matches all moments of x and y

claims.text: DP improves sample efficiency through dynamic synthetic data generation based on learner feedback
claims.verbatim_quote: we propose Deliberate Practice for Synthetic Data Generation (DP), a novel framework that improves sample efficiency through dynamic synthetic data generation

claims.text: DP efficiently approximates direct generation of informative samples rather than generate-then-prune
claims.verbatim_quote: DP efficiently approximates the direct generation of informative samples

claims.text: DP generates 3.4x fewer samples and 6x fewer iterations on ImageNet-100 with superior performance
claims.verbatim_quote: On ImageNet-100, DP generates 3.4x fewer samples and requires six times fewer iterations

claims.text: DP generates 8x fewer samples with 30% fewer iterations on ImageNet-1k
claims.verbatim_quote: on ImageNet-1k, it generates 8x fewer samples with a 30% reduction in iterations

claims.text: DP improves OOD performance by up to 15%, even outperforming models trained on real data
claims.verbatim_quote: DP exhibits strong performance on out-of-distribution (OOD) datasets, even outperforming models trained with real data

claims.text: Entropy-guided sampling uses learner's prediction entropy to generate challenging examples
claims.verbatim_quote: By leveraging the learner's prediction entropy to guide the generation process, our approach generates only the most challenging and informative training examples

claims.text: Patience mechanism triggers new data generation when validation accuracy plateaus
claims.verbatim_quote: If no improvement is observed for T_max intervals (patience threshold), the framework triggers new data generation

claims.text: RMT provides theoretical justification that selecting hard examples improves scaling laws
claims.verbatim_quote: Our results show that selecting hard examples improves scaling laws, providing theoretical justification for our approach

claims.text: DP uses LDM1.5 as the pre-trained text-to-image model for generating synthetic data
claims.verbatim_quote: For all the experiments, we use the LDM1.5 as the pre-trained text-to-image (T2I) model

claims.text: DP trains a ViT-B classifier with synthetic data in the scaling experiments
claims.verbatim_quote: We train a Vision Transformer (ViT-B) classifier with synthetic data

claims.text: DP leverages DDIMs for efficient sampling with entropy guidance
claims.verbatim_quote: We leverage denoising diffusion implicit models (DDIMs) for efficient sampling

claims.text: Training on informative examples improves scaling laws of synthetic data
claims.verbatim_quote: We theoretically show how training on challenging, informative examples improves scaling laws

claims.text: DP uses classifier-free guidance with coefficient lambda to control conditional sampling strength
claims.verbatim_quote: epsilon_theta(x_t, y) approx (1 + lambda)epsilon_tilde_theta(x, y) - lambda epsilon_tilde_theta(x)

claims.text: Naively adding synthetic data leads to diminishing returns; pruning improves scaling
claims.verbatim_quote: Prior work has shown that scaling synthetic data is inherently challenging, as naively adding new data leads to diminishing returns

claims.text: The paper addresses expert overlap caused by auxiliary load balancing loss that reduces expert distinctiveness
claims.verbatim_quote: the commonly used auxiliary load balancing loss often leads to expert overlap and overly uniform routing, which hinders expert specialization

claims.text: The orthogonality loss encourages experts to process distinct types of tokens by penalizing similarity between expert representations
claims.verbatim_quote: To foster expert specialization, we aim to make the representations learned by different experts for the same input token as independent as possible

claims.text: The variance loss encourages more discriminative routing decisions by enhancing variance in routing scores
claims.verbatim_quote: We introduce a variance-based loss to encourage more diverse routing decisions and promote expert specialization

claims.text: The method improves classic MoE baselines with auxiliary loss by up to 23.79% on downstream tasks without architectural changes
claims.verbatim_quote: our method improves classic MoE baselines with auxiliary loss by up to 23.79%, while also maintaining load balancing in downstream tasks, without any architectural modifications

claims.text: The orthogonality loss L_o and variance loss L_v are theoretically compatible and mutually reinforcing with the auxiliary loss
claims.verbatim_quote: we demonstrate that L_o and L_v are compatible in their optimization directions within MoE, then show that they mutually reinforce each other

claims.text: The method is evaluated on DeepSeek-MoE-16B, Moonlight, and other MoE architectures across 11 benchmarks
claims.verbatim_quote: We evaluate our method on a total of 11 benchmarks

claims.text: The proposed objectives are gradient-level compatible with the auxiliary loss and contribute to optimizing the training process
claims.verbatim_quote: Gradient-level analysis demonstrates that these objectives are compatible with the existing auxiliary loss and contribute to optimizing the training process

claims.text: The orthogonality loss reduces expert overlap by up to 45% compared to baseline methods
claims.verbatim_quote: Our joint objectives reduce expert overlap by up to 45%

claims.text: The variance loss increases routing score variance by over 150%, leading to clearer specialization
claims.verbatim_quote: increase routing score variance by over 150%, leading to clearer specialization and more discriminative expert assignment

claims.text: The combined loss integrates auxiliary loss, orthogonality loss, and variance loss
claims.verbatim_quote: L = L_h + L_balance, L_balance = alpha * L_aux + beta * L_o + gamma * L_v

claims.text: Expert overlap arises because the auxiliary loss is independent of expert parameter matrices, causing gradient interference
claims.verbatim_quote: Introduction of the auxiliary loss function leads to a more homogenized distribution of tokens across experts, which may reduce the distinctiveness of each expert

claims.text: As training progresses, routing output becomes more uniform due to the auxiliary loss optimizing for balanced f_j
claims.verbatim_quote: As training progresses, the routing output tends to become more uniform, with the expert weight distribution gradually converging towards an equal allocation

claims.text: In post-training, data distributions are narrower and more domain-specific, intensifying the tension between balanced routing and expert specialization
claims.verbatim_quote: While load balancing is effective in avoiding idle experts during large-scale pre-training, it often hinders model adaptation in the post-training stage for downstream tasks

claims.text: Training dynamics of diffusion models display implicit dynamical regularization that prevents memorization even in highly overparameterized settings.
claims.verbatim_quote: the training dynamics of neural network-based score functions display a form of implicit regularization that prevents memorization even in highly overparameterized diffusion models.

claims.text: tau_mem increases linearly with training set size n, while tau_gen remains constant, creating a growing generalization window that widens linearly with n.
claims.verbatim_quote: tau_mem increases linearly with the training set size n, while tau_gen remains constant. This creates a growing window of training times with n where models generalize effectively

claims.text: Experiments use U-Net score models on CelebA dataset, varying training set size n from 128 to 32768.
claims.verbatim_quote: We conduct our experiments on the CelebA face dataset, which we convert to grayscale downsampled images of size d=32x32, and vary the training set size n from 128 up to 32768. Our score model has a U-Net architecture

claims.text: In the RFNN model, tau_gen relates to the bulk rho_2 (order 1/Delta_t, independent of n) and tau_mem relates to the bulk rho_1 (scaling as psi_n/Delta_t, with n).
claims.verbatim_quote: The timescale tau_gen on which the first relaxation takes place is associated to the formation of the generalization regime. It is related to the bulk rho_2 and is of order 1/Delta_t. The timescale tau_mem, on which the second stage of the dynamics takes place, is associated to overfitting and memorization. It is related to the bulk rho_1, and scales as psi_p/Delta_t*lambda_min

claims.text: The key mechanism behind the widening gap between tau_gen and tau_mem is the irregularity of the empirical score at low noise level and large n.
claims.verbatim_quote: the key mechanism behind the widening gap between tau_gen and tau_mem is the irregularity of the empirical score at low noise level and large n.

claims.text: When n is larger than n*(p) (model-dependent threshold), overfitting disappears at infinite training times due to architectural regularization.
claims.verbatim_quote: It is only when n becomes larger than a model-dependent threshold that overfitting disappears at infinite training times.

claims.text: A model without regularization achieving zero training loss only learns the empirical score, bound to reproduce training samples at the end of the backward process.
claims.verbatim_quote: A model without regularization achieving zero training loss only learns the empirical score, and is bound to reproduce samples of the training dataset at the end of the backward process.

claims.text: Implicit dynamical regularization from training dynamics substantially enlarges the generalization regime beyond what architectural regularization alone provides.
claims.verbatim_quote: Our results shows that implicit dynamical regularization in training plays a crucial role in score-based generative models, substantially enlarging the generalization regime

claims.text: The empirical score contains a low frequency part close to the population score and a high-frequency part that is dataset-dependent, with the latter learned only at very long training times.
claims.verbatim_quote: the empirical score contains a low frequency part that is close to the population score, and a high-frequency part that is dataset-dependent

claims.text: tau_gen is independent of n and approximately 100K SGD steps for U-Nets with W=32 on CelebA.
claims.verbatim_quote: the FID quickly decreases to reach a minimum value on a timescale tau_gen (approx 100K) that does not depend on n.

claims.text: In the RFNN model, the generalization loss L_gen decreases as n increases, and for n > n*(p) = p the model is no longer expressive enough to memorize.
claims.verbatim_quote: As n increases, the asymptotic generalization loss L_gen decreases, indicating a reduced overfitting. For n > n*(p) = p, although some overfitting remains, the model is no longer expressive enough to memorize the training data

claims.text: For n=1024 on CelebA, generated samples at tau=100K steps clearly differ from their nearest neighbors, indicating correct generalization, while at tau=1.62M steps they are very close to training samples.
claims.verbatim_quote: the generated samples at tau=100K clearly differ from their nearest neighbors in the training set, indicating that the model generalizes correctly.

claims.text: Critical windows emerge generically when the model localizes from larger to smaller sub-population
claims.verbatim_quote: we develop a simple, unifying theory to explain this phenomenon using the formalism of stochastic localization samplers

claims.text: Stochastic localization framework encompasses both diffusion and autoregressive models
claims.verbatim_quote: Our theory (1) applies to autoregressive and diffusion models; (2) makes no distributional assumptions

claims.text: Autoregressive models are a special case of stochastic localization samplers
claims.verbatim_quote: In autoregressive models, the degradation is the masking of entries from right to left

claims.text: Critical windows in LLMs manifest as sudden shifts in behavior like jailbreaks and reasoning failures
claims.verbatim_quote: Large language models can exhibit unexpected behavior in the blink of an eye

claims.text: Critical windows coincide with failures in problem solving for math and reasoning benchmarks
claims.verbatim_quote: critical windows often coincide with failures in problem solving for various math and reasoning benchmarks

claims.text: Forward-reverse experiment noises and denoises samples to understand critical windows
claims.verbatim_quote: we study the forward-reverse experiment, where we noise and denoise samples with a given attribute to understand critical windows

claims.text: Jailbreaks occur in first few generated tokens; theory explains when they occur
claims.verbatim_quote: Our theory provides a simple explanation for when jailbreaks occur: when the unaligned component assigns a much higher probability to the current text than the aligned component

claims.text: Critical windows experiments on LLAMA-3.1-8B-Instruct across 7 math/reasoning benchmarks
claims.verbatim_quote: we empirically demonstrate critical windows for generations from LLAMA-3.1-8B-Instruct, Phi-3-7B-Instruct, and Qwen-2.5-7B-Instruct on 7 different math and reasoning benchmarks

claims.text: Features of generation are modeled as mixture model components; critical windows arise when model localizes to sub-mixture
claims.verbatim_quote: To capture the notion of a feature of the generation, we assume that the distribution X ~ p is a mixture model

claims.text: Critical windows in in-context learning connect to all-or-nothing phenomenon in statistical inference
claims.verbatim_quote: we identify an intriguing connection to the all-or-nothing phenomenon from statistical inference

claims.text: A single trajectory can contain multiple critical windows, each splitting sub-populations
claims.verbatim_quote: a single trajectory can contain multiple critical windows, each splitting a sub-population into smaller sub-populations

claims.text: Autoregressive critical windows describe in-context learning with sample complexity in terms of T_after^C
claims.verbatim_quote: Autoregressive critical windows can also be applied to describe in-context learning

claims.text: Autoregressive models can support deeper hierarchies than diffusion models
claims.verbatim_quote: We also use our theory to argue that autoregressive models can support deeper hierarchies than diffusion models

claims.text: Stochastic localization sampler generates X by reversing the forward observation process
claims.verbatim_quote: the associated stochastic localization sampler is the algorithm that generates a sample for X by first sampling Y_tm

claims.text: With two isotropic Gaussians, critical window appears around ln ||mu|| with width independent of dimension
claims.verbatim_quote: We show that with two isotropic Gaussians, the critical window appears around a single point, ln ||mu||, with width independent of the dimension

claims.text: The paper introduces temporal difference flows, a generative modeling approach for long-horizon predictive models of state using the successor measure
claims.verbatim_quote: we introduced temporal difference flows, a novel generative modeling approach that significantly advances long-horizon predictive models of state

claims.text: TD^2-CFM exploits the linearity of the Bellman equation for a more structured approach, resulting in lower gradient variance
claims.verbatim_quote: To formulate a more structured approach, we exploit the linearity of the Bellman equation

claims.text: TD-CFM learns from offline datasets containing only one-step transitions through an iterative bootstrapping process
claims.verbatim_quote: we can instead learn from an offline dataset rho containing only one-step transitions (S, A, S') through an iterative process similar to (3)

claims.text: The TD-based flow and diffusion approaches achieve better long-horizon predictive models compared to GAN and VAE baselines
claims.verbatim_quote: We now present a series of experiments to assess the efficacy of our TD-based flow and diffusion approaches with baselines employing Generative Adversarial Networks and beta-Variational Auto-Encoders

claims.text: TD-flow methods improve performance over zero-shot FB policies when planning with Generalized Policy Improvement
claims.verbatim_quote: Performance improvement over the zero-shot Forward Backward policies when planning with Generalized Policy Improvement

claims.text: TD-CFM has higher gradient variance than TD^2-CFM due to additional covariance from the conditional target
claims.verbatim_quote: sigma_TD-CFM^2 = sigma_TD^2-CFM^2 + gamma^2 E_rho[ Tr( Cov_{X_1|S,A,X_t}[ ... ] ) ]

claims.text: Experiments were conducted with a subset of domains from the DeepMind Control Suite
claims.verbatim_quote: Experiments in this paper were conducted with a subset of domains from the DeepMind Control Suite

claims.text: TD^2-DD splits the DDPM loss into one-step and bootstrap terms, analogous to the TD^2 approach in flow matching
claims.verbatim_quote: we can split our DDPM loss into two terms, one that will use standard DDPM training on one-step transitions and the second term that will regress to our target networks noise prediction

claims.text: The successor measure satisfies the Bellman equation m^pi = T^pi m^pi, enabling iterative approximation
claims.verbatim_quote: m^pi(cdot | s, a) = (T^pi m^pi) (cdot | s, a)

claims.text: REG entangles image latents with a class token from pretrained foundation models to provide discriminative guidance during denoising.
claims.verbatim_quote: We propose a straightforward method called Representation Entanglement for Generation (REG), which entangles low-level image latents with a single high-level class token from pretrained foundation models for denoising.

claims.text: REG achieves 63x faster training convergence than SiT and 23x faster than REPA on ImageNet 256x256.
claims.verbatim_quote: SiT-XL/2 + REG demonstrates remarkable convergence acceleration, achieving 63x and 23x faster training than SiT-XL/2 and SiT-XL/2 + REPA

claims.text: REG achieves 1.8 FID on ImageNet 256x256 at 4M training iterations with SiT-XL/2.
claims.verbatim_quote: At 4M steps, REG achieves a record-low FID of 1.8

claims.text: REG entangles a DINOv2 class token with image latents via synchronized noise injection and spatial concatenation.
claims.verbatim_quote: REG entangles low-level image latents with a single high-level class token from pretrained foundation models, achieved via synchronized noise injection and spatial concatenation.

claims.text: REG builds on SiT, adding a class token branch with separate velocity prediction loss to enable discriminative guidance.
claims.verbatim_quote: We inject noise into both the class token and image latents as a paired input for the SiT forward process

claims.text: REG adds less than 0.5% FLOPs and latency overhead while achieving 56.46% FID reduction.
claims.verbatim_quote: REG introduces only a marginal increase in parameter count (+0.30%) and FLOPs (+0.38%) relative to REPA

claims.text: SiT-L/2 + REG at 400K iterations outperforms SiT-XL/2 + REPA at 4M iterations, using 10x less training.
claims.verbatim_quote: SiT-L/2 + REG trained for merely 400K iterations outperforms SiT-XL/2 + REPA trained for 4M iterations

claims.text: REG's inference jointly reconstructs image latents and global semantics, with semantic knowledge actively guiding generation.
claims.verbatim_quote: REG jointly reconstructs both image latents and corresponding global semantics from random noise initialization. It acquired semantic knowledge actively guides and enhances the generation quality.

claims.text: REG consistently achieves higher CKNNA scores than REPA and SiT across training steps, layers, and timesteps.
claims.verbatim_quote: REG consistently outperforms REPA in both metrics throughout training, demonstrating its superior capacity for discriminative semantic learning

claims.text: REG combines velocity prediction loss L_pred with REPA-style alignment loss L_REPA, weighted by hyperparameters beta and lambda.
claims.verbatim_quote: The final training loss integrates both prediction and alignment objectives, where lambda governs the relative weight

claims.text: Class token entanglement alone (without alignment) reduces FID from 33.0 to 26.67 on SiT-B/2, a 19.18% improvement.
claims.verbatim_quote: class token entanglement alone consistently enhances generation quality, with FID improvements ranging from 0.95 to 6.33 points

claims.text: TD^2-CFM converges to the true successor measure via contraction of the Bellman operator in Wasserstein distance
claims.verbatim_quote: m_1^{(n)} -> m^pi by the contraction property of T^pi

claims.text: EMD between samples from the true occupancy and the GHM provides an estimate of distribution distance
claims.verbatim_quote: the Earth Mover's Distance (EMD) between samples from the true occupancy and our GHM which provides an estimate of the distance between these two probability distributions

claims.text: The GHM approximates the successor measure m^pi which enables value function computation via expectation over rewards
claims.verbatim_quote: Q^pi(s,a) = (1-gamma)^{-1} E_{X ~ m^pi(cdot|s,a)}[r(X)]

claims.text: REPA's external alignment is absent during denoising inference, limiting discriminative information utilization.
claims.verbatim_quote: the external alignment of REPA, which is absent during the entire denoising inference process, falls short of fully harnessing the potential of discriminative information

claims.text: DINOv2-B achieves the best performance among target representations with FID 15.22 on SiT-B/2 at 400K iterations.
claims.verbatim_quote: Among all configurations, DINOv2-B achieves the best performance with the lowest FID (15.22) and highest IS (94.64).

claims.text: DeFoG addresses the tight coupling between training and sampling in diffusion-based graph generative models
claims.verbatim_quote: Diffusion-based graph generative models achieve superior performance but face inefficient sampling and limited flexibility due to the tight coupling between training and sampling stages

claims.text: DeFoG leverages a discrete flow matching formulation with linear interpolation noising and CTMC denoising tailored to graphs
claims.verbatim_quote: DeFoG leverages a discrete flow matching (DFM) inspired formulation that we tailor to graph settings

claims.text: DeFoG achieves near-saturated validity of 99.5% on planar graphs
claims.verbatim_quote: DeFoG achieves SOTA performance across diverse datasets, with near-saturated validity of 99.5%, 96.5%, and 90% on planar, tree, and SBM datasets

claims.text: DeFoG achieves 92.8% validity on MOSES, surpassing the previous SOTA of 90.5%
claims.verbatim_quote: it achieves 92.8% validity on MOSES, surpassing the previous SOTA of 90.5%

claims.text: DeFoG uses RRWP features to address model expressivity limitations and ensure permutation equivariance
claims.verbatim_quote: ensuring node permutation equivariance and addressing the model expressivity limitations inherent to this data modality

claims.text: The CTMC is characterized by an initial distribution p_0 and a rate matrix R_t that governs evolution across time
claims.verbatim_quote: a CTMC is characterized by an initial distribution, p_0, and a rate matrix, R_t, that governs its evolution across time t in [0,1]

claims.text: DeFoG is evaluated on QM9 for molecular graph generation considering both implicit and explicit hydrogens
claims.verbatim_quote: For the molecular generation tasks, we examine the results for QM9, considering both implicit and explicit hydrogens

claims.text: DeFoG uses independent Euler steps for each node and edge in a CTMC denoising process that respects graph structure
claims.verbatim_quote: the denoising process requires simulating a CTMC, driven by its rate matrix R_t

claims.text: Minimizing the cross-entropy loss directly corresponds to minimizing an upper bound on the rate matrix estimation error
claims.verbatim_quote: Theorem 1 establishes that minimizing the cross-entropy (CE) loss directly corresponds to minimizing an upper bound on the rate matrix estimation error

claims.text: Time distortions during sampling can significantly affect performance by focusing on critical time intervals
claims.verbatim_quote: time distortions applied during the sampling stage can significantly affect performance

claims.text: Target guidance reinforces transition rates to the state predicted by the probabilistic model, controlled by hyperparameter omega
claims.verbatim_quote: The aim of target guidance is to reinforce the transition rate to the state predicted by the probabilistic model, z_1

claims.text: VideoJAM is a framework that instills an effective motion prior into video generators by encouraging joint appearance-motion representation learning.
claims.verbatim_quote: we introduce VideoJAM, a novel framework that instills an effective motion prior to video generators, by encouraging the model to learn a joint appearance-motion representation.

claims.text: VideoJAM extends the network to predict both pixels and optical flow from a single learned latent representation using two modified linear layers.
claims.verbatim_quote: we extend the objective to predict both the generated pixels and their corresponding motion from a single learned representation.

claims.text: Inner-Guidance steers generation toward coherent motion by leveraging the model's own evolving motion prediction as a dynamic guidance signal.
claims.verbatim_quote: we introduce Inner-Guidance, a mechanism that steers the generation toward coherent motion by leveraging the model's own evolving motion prediction as a dynamic guidance signal.

claims.text: VideoJAM achieves state-of-the-art performance in motion coherence, surpassing highly competitive proprietary models.
claims.verbatim_quote: VideoJAM achieves state-of-the-art performance in motion coherence, surpassing highly competitive proprietary models

claims.text: The original model is nearly invariant to random frame permutation until step 60 of generation, failing to distinguish valid from temporally incoherent video.
claims.verbatim_quote: the original model appears to be nearly invariant to frame shuffling until step 60 of the generation

claims.text: VideoJAM is extremely sensitive to temporal perturbations, with significant gap in loss between coherent and permuted videos.
claims.verbatim_quote: our model is extremely sensitive to these perturbations, as is indicated by the significant gap in the calculated loss

claims.text: VideoJAM uses optical flow as motion representation, represented as RGB video using angle and magnitude encoding.
claims.verbatim_quote: We opt to use optical flow since it is flexible, generic, and easily represented as an RGB video

claims.text: VideoJAM requires only two additional linear layers (W_in+ and W_out+) and fine-tunes all weights jointly.
claims.verbatim_quote: we only add two linear layers to the architecture

claims.text: VideoJAM-4B achieves 94.5% human preference in quality and 96.1% in motion vs CogVideo2B on VideoJAM-bench.
claims.verbatim_quote: VideoJAM-4B shows 94.5% human preference in quality and 96.1% in motion versus CogVideo2B

claims.text: VideoJAM improves motion VBench from 78.3 (DiT-4B) to 93.7 while maintaining appearance score (75.2 vs 75.1).
claims.verbatim_quote: VideoJAM improves motion VBench from 78.3 to 93.7 while maintaining appearance

claims.text: Inner-Guidance modifies the sampling distribution using the model's motion prediction as a dynamic signal, with weights w1=5 for text and w2=3 for motion.
claims.verbatim_quote: all experiments use w1=5, w2=3, where w=5 is the base model's text guidance scale

claims.text: VideoJAM extends the Flow Matching objective to predict both video and optical flow velocity from the joint representation.
claims.verbatim_quote: we extend the training objective to include an explicit motion term

claims.text: VideoJAM demonstrates effectiveness at both 4B and 30B parameter scales, addressing motion issues common across sizes.
claims.verbatim_quote: We consider two variants of the DiT text-to-video model, DiT-4B and DiT-30B, to demonstrate that motion coherence is a common issue for both small and large models

claims.text: The pixel reconstruction objective biases models toward appearance fidelity at the expense of motion coherence, nearly invariant to temporal perturbations.
claims.verbatim_quote: the conventional pixel reconstruction objective biases models toward appearance fidelity at the expense of motion coherence

claims.text: Lowest LPIPS 0.082 vs VITON-HD 0.117, HR-VTON 0.097 — more perceptually accurate.
claims.verbatim_quote: With the lowest LPIPS score (0.0820), it surpasses state-of-the-art models such as VITON-HD (0.117) and HR-VTON (0.097), indicating that our approach produces more perceptually accurate results.

claims.text: Best FID 7.782 — images closer to real in feature distribution and diversity.
claims.verbatim_quote: Additionally, our method achieves the best FID (7.782) and KID (1.53), suggesting that it generates images closer to real images in terms of feature distribution and diversity.

claims.text: Best KID 1.53.
claims.verbatim_quote: Additionally, our method achieves the best FID (7.782) and KID (1.53).

claims.text: SSIM 0.825 slightly lower vs LADI-VTON 0.864, HR-VTON 0.878 — trade-off for realism.
claims.verbatim_quote: While SSIM is slightly lower (0.825) compared to LADI-VTON (0.864) and HR-VTON (0.878), this reflects the trade-off between maintaining structural similarity and capturing more intricate details and textures.

claims.text: First integration of VQ-VAE into virtual try-on framework.
claims.verbatim_quote: Our first contribution is the integration of Vector Quantized Variational Autoencoders (VQ-VAEs) into the virtual try-on framework, marking their first use in this context.

claims.text: Shifts from text-based to image-based conditioning via CLIP image encoder.
claims.verbatim_quote: We shift the conditioning process from text-based to image-based input by using the CLIP image encoder instead of the CLIP text encoder employed in methods like LADI-VTON.

claims.text: Preserves fine-grained clothing details like logos and textures.
claims.verbatim_quote: Finally, we address a key challenge in virtual try-on — preserving fine-grained clothing details such as logos and textures — by introducing an Additional Feature Preserving Block.

claims.text: Image-based conditioning yields more accurate garment representation than text-based.
claims.verbatim_quote: By focusing on visual conditioning, we achieve a more accurate and realistic representation of garments in generated try-on images, aligning better with the needs of virtual try-on systems.

claims.text: GAN approaches: deform clothing to fit target body, blend with person image.
claims.verbatim_quote: GAN-based approaches have tackled the virtual try-on problem through two stages: deforming clothing to fit the target body and blending it with the person's image.

claims.text: VTON as exemplar-based inpainting: masked cloth-out person with 9-channel U-Net.
claims.verbatim_quote: Virtual try-on is framed as an exemplar-based image inpainting task, where the masked cloth-out person image is completed using the clothing image.

claims.text: LDM uses VAEs for latent-space diffusion, reducing resource requirements.
claims.verbatim_quote: Latent diffusion models (LDM) address this by using VAEs to perform diffusion in latent space, reducing resource requirements.

claims.text: Cross-attention integrates CLIP garment embeddings into DDPM denoising.
claims.verbatim_quote: This approach effectively combines person and clothing representations, enhancing the denoising process with contextual cross-attention for improved virtual try-on outputs.

claims.text: GAN models face misalignment artifacts with complex poses and occlusions.
claims.verbatim_quote: GAN-based models face persistent misalignment issues, leading to artifacts in reshaped clothing, especially with complex poses involving occlusions and deformations.

claims.text: LADI-VTON suffers spatial info loss limiting high-frequency detail like text/patterns.
claims.verbatim_quote: However, this approach suffers from spatial information loss, limiting its ability to preserve high-frequency details like text and patterns, which are essential for real-world scenarios.

claims.text: TryonDiffusion requires large paired datasets, computationally intensive.
claims.verbatim_quote: Tryon Diffusion employs two U-Nets for normal diffusion and feature preservation but requires large, paired datasets, making it computationally intensive.

claims.text: DDPM suffers from slow sampling speeds limiting practical use.
claims.verbatim_quote: While capable of producing diverse and high-quality images, DDPM suffers from slow sampling speeds, limiting its practical use.

claims.text: VITON TPS transformations struggled with large deformations like long sleeves.
claims.verbatim_quote: Early methods like VITON and CP-VITON used Thin Plate Spline (TPS) transformations in Geometric Matching Modules (GMM), but these struggled with large deformations like reshaping long sleeves.

claims.text: Text-to-image fails on intricate garment details; inpainting lacks precise control.
claims.verbatim_quote: Text-to-image methods fall short in capturing the intricate visual details of garments, and inpainting approaches struggle with precise control over modified areas.

claims.text: Diffusion framework significantly improves over GAN in facial features, textures, detail.
claims.verbatim_quote: By overcoming these challenges, our TryOn Framework accurately reproduces facial features, generates realistic textures, and preserves fine clothing details, making it a significant improvement over both GAN-based and diffusion-based methods.

claims.text: Additional Feature Preserving Block inspired by ControlNet for image generation control.
claims.verbatim_quote: The Additional Feature Preserving Block integrates external conditions into the architecture to enhance neural network performance. Inspired by ControlNet [26].

claims.text: Future: multimodal conditioning with visual+attribute inputs (garment dimensions, model characteristics).
claims.verbatim_quote: Future research could explore multimodal conditioning by incorporating both visual and attribute-based inputs, such as garment dimensions and model characteristics.

claims.text: Experiments use VITON-HD: 13,679 frontal-view women+top-clothing, 1024×768.
claims.verbatim_quote: Our experiments primarily utilize the VITON-HD dataset, which includes 13,679 pairs of frontal-view images of women and top clothing at a resolution of 1024×768.

claims.text: Inference uses DDPM with 50 sampling steps in both stages.
claims.verbatim_quote: For inference, we use the DDPM method [4] with 50 sampling steps.

claims.text: CLIP Vision Encoder replaces text-based conditioning for precise garment visual features.
claims.verbatim_quote: CLIP Vision Encoder replaces traditional text-based conditioning with image-based conditioning, directly utilizing visual garment features for more precise and realistic try-on outputs.

claims.text: Conditions Stable Diffusion with CLIP semantics for realistic garment rendering.
claims.verbatim_quote: By leveraging the visual semantics from CLIP and conditioning the Stable Diffusion network, the method achieves realistic and detailed garment rendering for virtual try-ons.

claims.text: 9-channel U-Net: noisy latent+masked latent+mask for diffusion-based inpainting.
claims.verbatim_quote: The U-Net input combines: (1) a noisy latent image, (2) the latent masked image, and (3) a mask, forming a 9-channel input.

claims.text: Diffusion U-Net uses CLIP garment embeddings via cross-attention for denoised prediction.
claims.verbatim_quote: The Diffusion U-Net predicts a denoised version of the input, leveraging the global condition integrated via cross-attention.

claims.text: ControlNet block enhances fine-grained features via additional cross-attention guidance.
claims.verbatim_quote: This step enhances fine-grained image features by incorporating additional cross-attention guidance, refining the overall output.

claims.text: Appearance Flow employed local pixel sampling for greater flexibility vs TPS-based methods.
claims.verbatim_quote: Later approaches, such as Appearance Flow, employed local pixel sampling for greater flexibility in handling complex deformations.

claims.text: CP-VITON improved alignment by incorporating segmentation maps.
claims.verbatim_quote: Methods like VTNFP and ACGPN improved alignment by incorporating segmentation maps.

claims.text: HR-VTON achieves SSIM 0.878 but our method achieves better LPIPS at 0.082.
claims.verbatim_quote: With the lowest LPIPS score (0.0820), it surpasses state-of-the-art models such as VITON-HD (0.117) and HR-VTON (0.097).

claims.text: Mask R-CNN generates clothing-agnostic person representation by removing garment information.
claims.verbatim_quote: Starting with a person image, a clothing-agnostic representation, called the masked cloth-out person image, is generated by removing garment information from the person.

claims.text: Appearance Flow offers greater flexibility in handling complex deformations compared to TPS-based GMM approaches.
claims.verbatim_quote: Later approaches, such as Appearance Flow, employed local pixel sampling for greater flexibility in handling complex deformations.

claims.text: TED-VITON builds on SD3 DiT architecture
claims.verbatim_quote: TED-VITON similarly builds on SD3 original checkpoints from official sources

claims.text: TED-VITON uses DiT-GarmentNet for garment features
claims.verbatim_quote: DiT-GarmentNet is designed to extract fine-grained garment features, including textures, patterns, fabric structures, logos

claims.text: TED-VITON uses GS-Adapter for high-order semantics
claims.verbatim_quote: the GS-Adapter uses the DINOv2 encoder to distill semantic garment information, including structure, style, and material

claims.text: TED-VITON uses text preservation loss for clarity
claims.verbatim_quote: we introduce a Text Preservation Loss to address common challenges in text and logo fidelity

claims.text: TED-VITON uses GPT-4o for garment descriptions
claims.verbatim_quote: Our approach uses GPT-4o to generate detailed garment descriptions that capture both basic and nuanced features

claims.text: TED-VITON uses DINOv2 for fine-grained garment details
claims.verbatim_quote: the GS-Adapter uses the DINOv2 encoder to distill semantic garment information

claims.text: TED-VITON migrates VTO to DiT-based architecture
claims.verbatim_quote: We propose TED-VITON, a novel VTO framework built on the DiT architecture to tackle critical challenges in garment detail fidelity and text clarity

claims.text: TED-VITON evaluated on VITON-HD
claims.verbatim_quote: We evaluate TED-VITON on the VITON-HD dataset and compare it with SOTA VTO methods

claims.text: TED-VITON evaluated on DressCode
claims.verbatim_quote: we test it on the DressCode upper-body dataset

claims.text: TED-VITON evaluated with FID
claims.verbatim_quote: we assess TED-VITON's performance in terms of image quality and realism using Frechet Inception Distance (FID) and Kernel Inception Distance (KID)

claims.text: TED-VITON uses DensePose for pose conditioning
claims.verbatim_quote: DensePose embedding E(Xpose) to align with the person's pose

claims.text: Text preservation loss uses DreamBooth-style prior preservation
claims.verbatim_quote: we introduce a prior preservation mechanism inspired by DreamBooth

claims.text: FIA-VTON built on inpainting version of SD v2.1
claims.verbatim_quote: FIA-VTON is built on an inpainting version of Stable Diffusion (SD)

claims.text: FIA-VTON replaces cross-attention with FIA modules
claims.verbatim_quote: all the cross-attention modules of the Denoising UNet are replaced by the Flow Infused Attention (FIA) modules

claims.text: FIA-VTON uses Flow Guider for dense flow estimation
claims.verbatim_quote: The Flow Guider estimates the dense warp flow F from the original garment image to the target garment in try-on image. In this paper we adopt the Dynamic Semantics Disentangling Module (DSDM) from D4-VTON

claims.text: FIA-VTON uses FashionCLIP as Spatial Guider
claims.verbatim_quote: We adopt FashionCLIP as the Spatial Guider in practice. FashionCLIP is fine-tuned on millions of image-text pairs of fashion clothes

claims.text: FIA-VTON proposes flow infused attention for implicit guidance
claims.verbatim_quote: we propose to leverage the implicit warp feature to guide the generation process, which adopts a Flow Infused Attention module in the diffusion model

claims.text: FIA-VTON outperforms D4-VTON
claims.verbatim_quote: DCI-VTON and D4-VTON perform worse than our method by 0.885 and 0.661 on the FIDu metric

claims.text: FIA-VTON evaluated on VITON-HD
claims.verbatim_quote: We present an evaluation of our proposed FIA-VTON method on two widely studied datasets

claims.text: FIA-VTON evaluated on DressCode
claims.verbatim_quote: We present an evaluation of our proposed FIA-VTON method on two widely studied datasets

claims.text: FIA-VTON evaluated with FID
claims.verbatim_quote: For both evaluation settings, we adopt a widely used metric: Frechet Inception Distance (FID)

claims.text: FIA-VTON uses Garment Net for local garment features
claims.verbatim_quote: On the other side, we feed the garment images Ig to the Garment Net to obtain the local garment feature in a single time step

claims.text: FIA module uses spatial attention from FashionCLIP
claims.verbatim_quote: The spatial embedding S is integrated with the enhanced model feature Pe by a cross-attention layer

claims.text: DiffusionTrend uses SDXL without retraining
claims.verbatim_quote: we apply our method to Stable Diffusion XL (SDXL)

claims.text: DiffusionTrend uses lightweight CNN for masks
claims.verbatim_quote: a lightweight, compact CNN is utilized to outline the clothing in both the model and garment images

claims.text: DiffusionTrend infuses garment latents at early denoising stage
claims.verbatim_quote: garment details are integrated into the reconstruction phase of the model image

claims.text: DiffusionTrend restores background at later stage
claims.verbatim_quote: we maintain the model's identity and background coherence by selectively replacing the latents in the background areas

claims.text: DiffusionTrend uses adaptive sampling termination
claims.verbatim_quote: we apply an adaptive sampling termination strategy based on perceptual color difference to automatically determine the optimal number of sampling steps

claims.text: DiffusionTrend forgoes retraining of diffusion models
claims.verbatim_quote: we introduce DiffusionTrend for virtual fashion try-on, which forgoes the need for retraining diffusion models

claims.text: DiffusionTrend evaluated on VITON-HD
claims.verbatim_quote: We conduct extensive experiments on two high-resolution datasets from the VITON benchmark: VITON-HD and DressCode

claims.text: DiffusionTrend evaluated on DressCode
claims.verbatim_quote: We conduct extensive experiments on two high-resolution datasets from the VITON benchmark: VITON-HD and DressCode

claims.text: DiffusionTrend evaluated with FID
claims.verbatim_quote: we use FID and KID to gauge the realism and fidelity of the synthesized images

claims.text: DiffusionTrend uses DDIM inversion for latent extraction
claims.verbatim_quote: These latents are then subjected to the DDIM Inversion process

claims.text: DiffusionTrend uses K-means for garment separation
claims.verbatim_quote: we perform K-means clustering upon the masked features

claims.text: EARSB enhances the overall image quality of virtual try-on by surgically targeting localized generation errors
claims.verbatim_quote: We propose a refinement model EARSB that surgically targets localized generation errors

claims.text: The human-to-garment model addresses limited data availability by generating synthetic paired training data
claims.verbatim_quote: we address the limited data availability by introducing a human-to-garment model that generates (human, synthetic garment) pairs from a single image of a clothed individual

claims.text: EARSB is evaluated on the VITON-HD dataset
claims.verbatim_quote: Experiments on VITON-HD and DressCode-Upper demonstrate that our synthetic data augmentation enhances the performance of prior work

claims.text: EARSB is evaluated on the DressCode-Upper dataset
claims.verbatim_quote: Experiments on VITON-HD and DressCode-Upper demonstrate that our synthetic data augmentation enhances the performance of prior work

claims.text: EARSB uses the WSC to obtain error maps that guide the adaptive noise schedule
claims.verbatim_quote: The trained WSC will predict an error map M for the initial image x1, which is then used to adjust the noise schedule in the diffusion process

claims.text: EARSB builds upon the I2SB framework for image-to-image refinement
claims.verbatim_quote: we adopt an Image-to-Image Schrodinger Bridge (I2SB) to learn the refinement of these regions in the generated images

claims.text: Synthetic data augmentation uses the H2G-UH dataset of upper-body pairs
claims.verbatim_quote: creating 12,730 synthetic pairs of upper-body human images (referred to as H2G-UH) and 8,939 pairs of full-body human images (referred to as H2G-FH)

claims.text: Synthetic data augmentation uses the H2G-FH dataset of full-body pairs
claims.verbatim_quote: creating 12,730 synthetic pairs of upper-body human images (referred to as H2G-UH) and 8,939 pairs of full-body human images (referred to as H2G-FH)

claims.text: EARSB splits into two expert denoisers for different denoising ranges
claims.verbatim_quote: a trained EARSB model is split into two models, each having an expert denoiser that is fine-tuned on denoising ranges t∈[0, 0.5] and t∈[0.5, 1]

claims.text: EARSB incorporates classifier guidance using the WSC for improved sampling
claims.verbatim_quote: our WSC guidance gives a direction toward the real data distribution

claims.text: The error-aware noise schedule outperforms the standard I2SB formulation
claims.verbatim_quote: When M is all ones in Eq. (5), our model reverts to the I2SB formulation

claims.text: The WSC is used both for error map generation and as classifier guidance
claims.verbatim_quote: the label for real data is 0 in WSC, the classifier guidance is

claims.text: CatV2TON handles both image and video try-on tasks with a single diffusion transformer model
claims.verbatim_quote: a simple and effective vision-based virtual try-on (V2TON) method that supports both image and video try-on tasks with a single diffusion transformer model

claims.text: CatV2TON uses Diffusion Transformer as its backbone, initialized from EasyAnimateV4
claims.verbatim_quote: we adopt DiT as our backbone and initialize our weights from EasyAnimateV4

claims.text: CatV2TON uses Adaptive Clip Normalization for maintaining temporal consistency in long video generation
claims.verbatim_quote: Adaptive Clip Normalization (AdaCN) is introduced to rectify segments based on guiding frames, thereby maintaining coherence in segmented long video generation

claims.text: CatV2TON is trained on the ViViD-S dataset for video try-on
claims.verbatim_quote: we present ViViD-S, a refined video try-on dataset that has undergone noise data reduction and quality enhancement

claims.text: CatV2TON uses overlapping clip-based inference for efficient long video generation
claims.verbatim_quote: we propose an overlapping clip-based inference strategy for long try-on video generation, utilizing preceding frames as guidance

claims.text: CatV2TON achieves strong results on VITON-HD dataset
CatV2TON achieves strong results on DressCode dataset
CatV2TON outperforms baselines on the ViViD-S test set
CatV2TON outperforms video try-on methods on the VVT dataset
CatV2TON uses DensePose as pose representation for motion continuity
claims.verbatim_quote: We use DensePose as the pose representation, which provides more detailed information compared to skeleton-based methods

claims.text: CatV2TON uses a lightweight Pose Encoder with only 89.90M trainable parameters
claims.verbatim_quote: The Pose Encoder is fully unlocked, with only 89.90 M trainable parameters, so the entire trainable portion accounts for less than 20% of the backbone network's total parameters

claims.text: CatV2TON uses 3D mask smoothing to enhance temporal consistency of masks
claims.verbatim_quote: we propose a 3D Mask Smoothing operation. This involves applying spatial and temporal average pooling to the clothing-agnostic masks followed by re-binarization

claims.text: ViViD-S is derived from the ViViD dataset by filtering back-facing frames
claims.verbatim_quote: We constructed the ViViD-S dataset based on the ViViD dataset

claims.text: EfficientVITON addresses challenges of realism and efficiency in virtual try-on using a pre-trained diffusion model with optimization
claims.verbatim_quote: this paper proposes EfficientVITON: a new virtual try-on system leveraging the impressive pre-trained Stable Diffusion model for better images and deployment feasibility

claims.text: EfficientVITON uses non-uniform timestep sampling to reduce computational overhead by 45.3% in training and 72.4% in inference
claims.verbatim_quote: significant reduces computational overhead (45.3% reduction in training time and 72.4% in inference time) without sacrificing visual fidelity

claims.text: EfficientVITON uses a spatial encoder to capture fine-grained clothing details
claims.verbatim_quote: The system includes a spatial encoder to maintain clothing's finer details and zero cross-attention blocks to capture the subtleties of how clothes fit a human body

claims.text: EfficientVITON uses zero cross-attention blocks for latent space semantic correspondence learning
claims.verbatim_quote: Zero Cross-Attention Blocks, Integrated within the U-Net decoder, these blocks learn the semantic correspondence between the clothing and the human body

claims.text: EfficientVITON achieves state-of-the-art results on the VITON-HD dataset
claims.verbatim_quote: Rigorous testing on the VITON-HD dataset, supplemented with real-world examples, has demonstrated that EfficientVITON achieves state-of-the-art results

claims.text: Non-uniform timestep sampling modifies the standard Stable Diffusion denoising process to concentrate steps at critical timesteps
claims.verbatim_quote: we apply a non-uniform distribution of a small number of timesteps in the denoising process

claims.text: EfficientVITON combines Stable Diffusion loss with Attention Total Variation Loss to refine attention maps
claims.verbatim_quote: EfficientVITON combines the Stable Diffusion loss (L_LDM) with an Attention Total Variation Loss (L_ATV) to refine attention maps

claims.text: EfficientVITON uses RePaint refinement for paired evaluations to improve performance
claims.verbatim_quote: Inference uses the PLMS sampler and, for paired evaluations, the RePaint approach

claims.text: ITVTON concatenates garment and person images along the width dimension to eliminate the need for extra encoders
claims.verbatim_quote: we avoid auxiliary image encoders by instead concatenating the garment and person images along the width dimension

claims.text: ITVTON incorporates integrated image-text descriptions using CLIP and T5-XXL encoders
claims.verbatim_quote: Two text encoders (CLIP ViT-L and T5-XXL) process textual prompts as input, enabling the generation of more controllable, high-quality images

claims.text: ITVTON restricts training to attention parameters within a Single-DiT block for parameter efficiency
claims.verbatim_quote: we restrict training to the attention parameters within a single Diffusion Transformer (Single-DiT) block

claims.text: ITVTON outperforms existing methods on VITON-HD across all metrics
ITVTON demonstrates robustness on 10,257 image pairs from the IGPair dataset
claims.verbatim_quote: experiments on 10,257 image pairs from IGPair confirm its robustness in real-world scenarios

claims.text: ITVTON uses the pre-trained FLUX.1-Fill-dev model as its foundation
claims.verbatim_quote: We utilize the pre-trained FLUX.1-Fill-dev model for training

claims.text: MFP-VTON is a mask-free framework for person-to-person VTON that generates high-fidelity fitting images
claims.verbatim_quote: we propose MFP-VTON, a Mask-Free framework for Person-to-Person VTON

claims.text: MFP-VTON employs FLUX-Fill-dev as its foundation model
claims.verbatim_quote: we employ Flux-Fill-dev as our foundation model. It is built upon diffusion transformers and introduces advanced inpainting and outpainting capabilities

claims.text: MFP-VTON uses Focus Attention loss to prioritize garment areas and non-garment details
claims.verbatim_quote: we introduce Focus Attention Loss, which helps the attention in transformer prioritize the garment on the reference person and the details outside the garment of the target person

claims.text: MFP-VTON constructs a custom person-to-person dataset using IDM for garment swapping
claims.verbatim_quote: we first use an off-the-shelf garment-to-person model to generate person-to-person paired data

claims.text: MFP-VTON is fine-tuned and evaluated on VITON-HD
claims.verbatim_quote: Our proposed model is fine-tuned on VITON-HD

claims.text: MFP-VTON draws inspiration from In-Context-LoRA for image concatenation along the width dimension
claims.verbatim_quote: We draw inspiration from In-Context-LoRA by concatenating multiple images directly along the width dimension to form the image condition

claims.text: VTON 360 uses SMPL-X normal maps as pseudo-3D pose representation for better geometric consistency across views
claims.verbatim_quote: We propose a novel pseudo-3D pose representation: the normal maps N derived from the SMPL-X model of the input body

claims.text: VTON 360 uses Multi-view Spatial Attention to model correlations between features from different viewing angles via a correlation matrix
claims.verbatim_quote: We design a Multi-view Spatial Attention mechanism that models the correlations between features from different viewing angles, featuring a novel correlation matrix modeling the relationships among different input views

claims.text: VTON 360 uses multi-view CLIP embedding to incorporate camera information into garment features
claims.verbatim_quote: We propose a multi-view CLIP embedding that enhances the garment CLIP embedding used in 2D VTON methods with camera information

claims.text: VTON 360 is evaluated on Thuman2.0 dataset achieving DINOsim 0.633
VTON 360 achieves DINOsim 0.623 on MVHumanNet dataset
VTON 360 significantly outperforms DreamWaltz in DINO similarity and user preference
VTON 360 achieves higher DINOsim (0.633) compared to TIP-Editor (0.569) on Thuman2.0
VTON 360 achieves higher DINOsim (0.633) compared to GaussCtrl (0.577) on Thuman2.0
VTON 360 uses Gaussian Splatting as its 3D representation for reconstruction
claims.verbatim_quote: We use Gaussian Splatting as our 3D representation

claims.text: Shining Yourself is the first to use diffusion models for virtual try-on of various ornaments
claims.verbatim_quote: we are the first to use diffusion models for virtual try-ons of various ornaments including bracelets, rings, earrings, and necklaces

claims.text: Shining Yourself uses an iterative pose-aware mask refinement to improve pose and scale alignments between ornaments and models
claims.verbatim_quote: We propose an iterative scheme to estimate a pose-aware wearing mask to significantly improve the pose and scale alignments between ornaments and models

claims.text: Shining Yourself uses mask-guided attention to preserve geometric feature structures by learning a mapping between reference and wearing masks
claims.verbatim_quote: By constraining attention to learning a mapping between reference and wearing masks, our method improves geometric feature preservation

claims.text: Shining Yourself builds upon the ReferenceNet module and Stable Diffusion for feature extraction from reference ornament images
claims.verbatim_quote: Our method is built upon the latent diffusion model (LDM) and ReferenceNet module

claims.text: Shining Yourself uses a custom 64k triplet dataset across four ornament categories

claims.text: Flow Generator Matching (FGM) is a specific type of Universal Inverse Distillation (UID)
claims.verbatim_quote: FGM is UID specifically applied to flow matching models

claims.text: RealUID is a specific type of Universal Inverse Distillation (UID)
claims.verbatim_quote: RealUID extends the UID framework to incorporate real data supervision

claims.text: AC-Mixer is a specific type of Auto-Compressing Networks (ACNs)
claims.verbatim_quote: AC-Mixer is an auto-compressing network variant specifically for MLP-Mixer architecture

claims.text: AC-BERT is a specific type of Auto-Compressing Networks (ACNs)
claims.verbatim_quote: AC-BERT is an auto-compressing network variant specifically for BERT architecture

claims.text: PLMS4 is a specific type of Adams-Bashforth / PLMS Methods
claims.verbatim_quote: PLMS4 is a specific 4th-order Pseudo Linear Multi-Step solver in the PLMS method family

claims.text: DiGress (Discrete Graph Diffusion) is a specific type of Diffusion Models (DMs)
claims.verbatim_quote: DiGress is a discrete denoising diffusion model specifically designed for graph generation

claims.text: DeFoG (Discrete Flow Matching on Graphs) is a specific type of Discrete Flow Matching (DFM)
claims.verbatim_quote: DeFoG is a specific application of discrete flow matching to the graph generation domain

claims.text: Self-grafting is a specific type of Grafting
claims.verbatim_quote: Self-grafting is a specific control experiment variant within the grafting methodology

claims.text: Interleaved Grafting is a specific type of Grafting
claims.verbatim_quote: Interleaved Grafting is a specific layer selection strategy in the grafting approach

claims.text: Lie-Trotter Splitting (LTSP) is a specific type of Splitting Numerical Methods
claims.verbatim_quote: LTSP is a specific splitting method for dividing guided ODEs into subproblems

claims.text: LLaDA 8B is a specific type of Masked Diffusion Models (MDMs)
claims.verbatim_quote: LLaDA 8B is a specific large-scale masked diffusion language model (8B parameters)

claims.text: TransE is a specific type of Knowledge Graph Embedding
claims.verbatim_quote: TransE is a specific translational embedding model within the knowledge graph embedding family

claims.text: Semantic Matching Energy (SME) is a specific type of Knowledge Graph Embedding
claims.verbatim_quote: SME is a specific neural knowledge graph embedding model using semantic matching energy

claims.text: VITON is a specific type of GAN-Based Virtual Try-On
claims.verbatim_quote: VITON is a specific GAN-based virtual try-on method using TPS warping

claims.text: CP-VITON is a specific type of GAN-Based Virtual Try-On
claims.verbatim_quote: CP-VITON is a specific GAN-based virtual try-on method using characteristic preservation

claims.text: HR-VTON is a specific type of GAN-Based Virtual Try-On
claims.verbatim_quote: HR-VTON is a specific GAN-based virtual try-on method for high-resolution results

claims.text: DDIM Inversion is a VTON-specific application of DDIM
claims.verbatim_quote: DDIM Inversion is the VTON application of DDIM sampling

claims.text: Adaptive Sampling Termination is a VTON-specific application of DDIM
claims.verbatim_quote: Adaptive sampling uses DDIM-based iterative refinement for VTON

claims.text: Artifact-Free Measurement-Guided Sampling is a VTON-specific application of Guided Diffusion Sampling
claims.verbatim_quote: Measurement-guided VTON sampling is guided diffusion applied to try-on

claims.text: Classifier Guidance is a VTON-specific application of Classifier-Free Guidance
claims.verbatim_quote: Classifier guidance is the older paradigm; CFG improved upon it

claims.text: Cross-Attention Conditioning is a VTON-specific application of Cross-Attention Conditioning
claims.verbatim_quote: Cross-attention in VTON denoising UNet

claims.text: Image-Based Conditioning is a VTON-specific application of Image-Based Conditioning
claims.verbatim_quote: Image conditioning for VTON vs text conditioning in theory

claims.text: Additional Feature Preserving Block is a VTON-specific application of Depth-Conditioned ControlNet
claims.verbatim_quote: VTON-specific ControlNet adaptation for garment feature preservation

claims.text: CatVTON achieves high-quality virtual try-on by simply concatenating garment and person images, eliminating extra encoders and ReferenceNet.
claims.verbatim_quote: CatVTON, a simple and efficient virtual try-on diffusion model that transfers in-shop or worn garments of arbitrary categories to target individuals by concatenating them along spatial dimensions as inputs of the diffusion model.

claims.text: Spatial concatenation feeds garment and person into the same denoising UNet, keeping them in shared latent space.
claims.verbatim_quote: If the garment and person features can be efficiently integrated within the shared latent space, is it possible to use a single UNet model to process both person and garment images simultaneously?

claims.text: VAE encodes garment and person images into latent space before spatial concatenation for the denoising UNet.
claims.verbatim_quote: The VAE encoder encodes the person and garment images into the latent space, optimizing computational efficiency during diffusion. Once encoded, the latent garment and person are concatenated in the spatial dimension as inputs to the denoising UNet.

claims.text: CatVTON uses CFG with 10% conditional dropout during training and CFG strength 2.5 at inference.
claims.verbatim_quote: We adopt a 10% conditional dropout to support classifier-free guidance (CFG) (Ho & Salimans, 2022) and employ the DREAM (Zhou et al., 2024) strategy during training.

claims.text: CFG strength 2.5-3.5 produces most realistic results; beyond 3.5 causes color distortion and noise.
claims.verbatim_quote: Increasing CFG strength enhances image detail and fidelity. However, beyond a strength of 3.5, the results developed severe color distortions and high-frequency noise, degrading visual quality. We found that a CFG strength between 2.5 and 3.5 produces the most realistic and natural results.

claims.text: DREAM with λ=10 best balances naturalness with detail fidelity in CatVTON training.
claims.verbatim_quote: We find that λ = 10 best balances naturalness with detail fidelity in our training.

claims.text: Small λ causes overly smooth images; large λ introduces excessive high-frequency details degrading naturalness.
claims.verbatim_quote: A small λ causes overly smooth images, while a large λ introduces excessive high-frequency details, reducing naturalness.

claims.text: Self-attention layers with global receptive field are the most critical component for adapting diffusion models to VTON.
claims.verbatim_quote: We find that self-attention modules with a global receptive field (Dosovitskiy et al., 2021) are the most critical part for try-on task with diffusion models, and achieve realistic try-on results by training only 49.57M parameters.

claims.text: Training only 49.57M self-attention parameters achieves comparable results to training full 815M UNet.
claims.verbatim_quote: Despite a significant disparity in the number of trainable parameters (815.45M, 267.24M, and 49.57M, respectively), all three variants produced satisfactory virtual try-on results, and no substantial differences are observed in visual quality and metrics.

claims.text: Removing cross-attention and text conditioning reduces 167M parameters; text conditions are detrimental to image-based try-on performance.
claims.verbatim_quote: We remove the text encoder and cross-attention modules in the UNet to further simplify the network and reduce 167.02M parameters. Our experiment reveals that training with text conditions leads to a detrimental impact on try-on performance.

claims.text: CatVTON eliminates ReferenceNet by concatenating garment and person spatially as single UNet input.
claims.verbatim_quote: Methods with ReferenceNet enhance detailed alignment in diffusion-based try-on by replicating weights from a denoising UNet and performing fine-tuning. However, this approach introduces additional trainable modules and increases the computational load. To address this, we concatenate person and garment images along the spatial dimension.

claims.text: Mask-free model trained on synthetic pairs from mask-based CatVTON output.
claims.verbatim_quote: For the training of the mask-free try-on model, we first leverage the already trained mask-based model to infer generated person images from randomly sampled person-garment pairs in the same datasets.

claims.text: CatVTON uses Stable Diffusion v1.5 inpainting (mask-based) and InstructPix2Pix (mask-free) as base models.
claims.verbatim_quote: We utilize the inpainting and InstructPix2Pix (Brooks et al., 2023) version of StableDiffusion v1.5 (Rombach et al., 2021) as the base models for the mask-based and mask-free try-on models, respectively.

claims.text: CatVTON evaluated on VITON-HD with 13,679 upper-body image pairs at 1024×768.
claims.verbatim_quote: Our experiments are conducted on three public datasets: VITON-HD (Choi et al., 2021), DressCode (Morelli et al., 2022), and DeepFashion (Ge et al., 2019).

claims.text: CatVTON evaluated on DressCode with 48,392 training pairs across upper/lower/dress categories.
claims.verbatim_quote: DressCode is composed of 48,392/5,400 training/testing pairs with full-body person images and in-shop upper, lower, and dresses.

claims.text: CatVTON achieves FID 5.425 (VITON-HD paired) and 3.992 (DressCode paired), outperforming all baselines.
claims.verbatim_quote: CatVTON achieves FID 5.425/3.992 on VITON-HD/DressCode under paired settings, outperforming IDM-VTON (5.762/6.821) and OOTDiffusion (9.305/4.610).

claims.text: CatVTON achieves LPIPS 0.0565 (VITON-HD) and 0.0455 (DressCode) in paired mask-based setting.
claims.verbatim_quote: CatVTON (Inpainting) achieves LPIPS 0.0565 on VITON-HD and 0.0455 on DressCode.

claims.text: Deploying high-res VTON on terminal devices like smartphones remains unexplored and requires further work.
claims.verbatim_quote: Deploying high-resolution image generation models on terminal devices, such as smartphones, remains an area that requires further exploration.

claims.text: Pre-trained image encoders like DINOv2 and CLIP are not optimized for detail preservation — critical for VTON.
claims.verbatim_quote: Pre-trained image encoders like DINOv2 and CLIP are not optimized for detail preservation—a crucial factor in virtual try-on applications.

claims.text: CatVTON eliminates the need for ControlNet-like external condition modules by using only garment+person as input.
claims.verbatim_quote: Numerous try-on methods depend on extra preprocessing such as pose estimation, human parsing, and captioning to guide the try-on process. We think the garment and person images contain sufficient information for try-ons.

claims.text: CatVTON trained with AdamW optimizer: batch 128, constant lr=1e-5, 16K steps at 512×384.
claims.verbatim_quote: The AdamW (Loshchilov & Hutter, 2019) optimizer is employed with a batch size of 128 and a constant learning rate of 1e-5 for 16,000 steps training under 512×384 resolution.

claims.text: OOTDiffusion generates realistic controllable VTON via outfitting fusion in self-attention layers without warping.
claims.verbatim_quote: We present OOTDiffusion, a novel network architecture for realistic and controllable image-based virtual try-on (VTON).

claims.text: Leverages pretrained latent diffusion models to ensure high realism and natural try-on effects.
claims.verbatim_quote: We leverage the power of pretrained latent diffusion models, designing an outfitting UNet to learn the garment detail features.

claims.text: Garment features precisely aligned with target human body via outfitting fusion in self-attention layers without redundant warping.
claims.verbatim_quote: Without a redundant warping process, the garment features are precisely aligned with the target human body via the proposed outfitting fusion in the self-attention layers of the denoising UNet.

claims.text: Outfitting dropout enables CFG with respect to garment features — adjusting garment control strength via guidance scale.
claims.verbatim_quote: We introduce outfitting dropout to the training process, which enables us to adjust the strength of the garment features through classifier-free guidance.

claims.text: OOTDiffusion is an extension of Stable Diffusion, the most commonly-used latent diffusion model.
claims.verbatim_quote: Our OOTDiffusion is an extension of Stable Diffusion, which is one of the most commonly-used latent diffusion models.

claims.text: SD employs VAE encoder/decoder for latent space image representations.
claims.verbatim_quote: Stable Diffusion employs a variational autoencoder (VAE) that consists of an encoder E and a decoder D to enable image representations in the latent space.

claims.text: UNet ϵ_θ trained to denoise Gaussian noise with conditioning via MSE loss.
claims.verbatim_quote: A UNet ϵ_θ is trained to denoise a Gaussian noise ϵ with a conditioning input encoded by a CLIP text encoder.

claims.text: Conditioning input τ_θ(y) correlated with denoising UNet via cross-attention mechanism.
claims.verbatim_quote: The conditioning input τ_θ(y) is correlated with the denoising UNet by the cross-attention mechanism.

claims.text: Outfitting UNet learns garment detail features in a single step, operating parallel to denoising UNet.
claims.verbatim_quote: We design an outfitting UNet to learn the detail features of garments in the latent space in a single step.

claims.text: Strength of garment control adjusted via guidance scale — enhancing VTON controllability.
claims.verbatim_quote: Through this approach, the strength of garment control over the generated result can be simply adjusted by a guidance scale, which further enhances the controllability of our VTON method.

claims.text: CLIP-based textual inversion fails to preserve fine garment details due to CLIP encoder bottleneck.
claims.verbatim_quote: Neither of these methods succeeded in fully preserving garment details like complicated patterns and text due to the information loss caused by the CLIP encoder.

claims.text: Trained on VITON-HD with 13,679 upper-body clothing image pairs.
claims.verbatim_quote: We train our OOTDiffusion on two broadly-used high-resolution benchmark datasets, i.e., VITON-HD and Dress Code.

claims.text: Trained on DressCode supporting upper/lower/dress garment categories.
claims.verbatim_quote: We train our OOTDiffusion on two broadly-used high-resolution benchmark datasets, i.e., VITON-HD and Dress Code, respectively.

claims.text: SOTA structural similarity on VITON-HD and DressCode.
claims.verbatim_quote: Extensive qualitative and quantitative evaluations demonstrate our superiority over the state-of-the-art VTON methods.

claims.text: OOTDiffusion achieves controllability via outfitting dropout without extra architectural blocks.
claims.verbatim_quote: ControlNet and T2I-Adapter incorporated additional blocks into pretrained diffusion models to enable spatial conditioning controls.
